article.title: Entrapment neuropathies: a contemporary approach to pathophysiology, clinical assessment, and management

source_locator.role: article identifier and human-facing metadata locator only; not an article-body source

authorized_source.normalization: The authorized XML ended with a closing angle bracket and no newline. One terminal LF was added for repository text normalization; both remote and local digests are recorded.

excluded_routes: United States PMC article and PDF endpoints
Europe PMC generated PDF and supplementary routes
all external figure, thumbnail, and image-table binaries

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.copyright_statement: Copyright © 2020 The Author(s). Published by Wolters Kluwer Health, Inc. on behalf of The International Association for the Study of Pain.
license.source_license_scope: The JATS states that unrestricted use, distribution, and reproduction are permitted in any medium when the original work is properly cited.
license.third_party_component_policy: No figure, thumbnail, image-based table, source visual layout, or other external binary is emitted in the training Markdown. Figure 2 is explicitly described as adapted from reference 55 and was excluded.

review_method.automation_boundary: XML parsing was used only to navigate metadata, sections, captions, identifiers, and integrity properties. The direct-training Markdown was manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, clinically bounded, and checked against the JATS. Raw extraction was not emitted as training prose.
reviewed_regions: front matter, identifiers, abstracts, and license
neuropathic-pain grading and every assessment subsection
natural history, prognosis, and management at exclusion-disposition level
conclusion, disclosures, footnotes, and references at disposition level
all four figure captions and the image-based table caption at disposition level

figures.subject: potential pathomechanisms and risk factors
figures.rights_note: No separate credit is visible in the JATS caption; exclusion avoids unnecessary component-level rights and expression risk.

figures.subject: neuropathic-pain grading system
figures.rights_note: The JATS caption explicitly says the system was adapted from reference 55; the visual was excluded as third-party/adapted content.

figures.subject: bedside sensory testing tools
figures.rights_note: Excluded both for visual-content review and because it could facilitate unsupervised self-testing.

figures.subject: multidimensional patient profiles and proposed personalized management
figures.rights_note: Excluded because the management graphic is outside the corpus scope and unnecessary to the retained heterogeneity finding.

tables.subject: differential diagnoses and serious pathologies potentially resembling carpal tunnel syndrome or sciatica
tables.jats_representation: external JPEG rather than structured table text
tables.rights_note: No separate credit is visible in the JATS caption. The image was excluded and was not reconstructed as a lay screening checklist.

evidence_audit.central_design_limit: The article narratively combines preclinical models, human observational findings, diagnostic-performance studies, clinical guidelines, trials, and other reviews without a systematic evidence-selection or uniform bias-assessment method.
evidence_audit.human_preclinical_boundary: Much mechanistic evidence comes from acute severe animal or experimental nerve-injury models, while the common human entrapments discussed may be slower, milder, and chronic. Experimental findings cannot define a safe rope exposure.
presentation_boundaries: pain is not synonymous with demonstrable nerve damage
radicular pain is not synonymous with radiculopathy
mechanosensitivity is neither necessary nor sufficient for a nerve lesion
anatomical distribution can be informative without being perfectly canonical
test_limitations: early carpal tunnel involvement may escape nerve-conduction detection
nerve-conduction value is less established for some proximal conditions
MRI can produce false positives and false negatives
the imaging level may not match the clinical examination
ultrasound nerve size is one finding rather than a standalone diagnosis
test meaning depends on condition, stage, population, protocol, reference standard, and interpreter
excluded_source_values_and_claims: treatment effects, recommendations, and selection criteria
medication, injection, therapy, and surgery claims

clinical_boundary.qualified_evaluation_required: Numbness, tingling, altered sensation, electric or burning pain, weakness, loss of coordination, or other neurological symptoms after rope require evaluation by a qualified medical professional.
forbidden_derivations: safe rope placement or body location
safe contact width, loading direction, or geometry
direct claims about bondage safety or technique
clinical_boundary.transfer_rule: The article did not study rope bondage, acute rope compression, suspension, wraps, scene monitoring, release, or return to rope. Its clinical and experimental findings cannot be converted into operational rope guidance.

retained_scope: symptom, sign, diagnosis, and test distinctions
professional assessment scope and test limitations
strict no-threshold and no-management rope-transfer boundary
excluded_sensitive_or_unsafe_material: treatment, prognosis, recovery, or self-care advice
external figures, thumbnails, and image-based table

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign PMCID PMC7382548 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
